key_insight: The paper turns hard topological constraints like Betti numbers and persistent homology into differentiable Hodge-spectral surrogates using low-frequency Laplacian filters.
engineering_relevance: This enables gradient-based optimization of graphs and point clouds with topology-aware objectives that are smoother and easier to integrate into machine learning and design pipelines.
method: Build soft graph and soft clique-complex Hodge Laplacians that interpolate to the ordinary Hodge Laplacian in the hard limit.
Use heat, resolvent, and polynomial spectral filters to represent homological information through zero and near-zero modes.
Define differentiable losses for topology-constrained optimization and combine them with standard geometric or graph-feature objectives.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that Hodge spectral-filter losses on point clouds give more spatially distributed gradients, smoother scale-normalized behavior, and geometry-aware updates than persistent-homology-based losses.
watch_item: The approach relies on spectral relaxations and normalization choices, so performance may depend on how softly the complex is constructed and how near-zero modes are separated from noise.
paper_title: Hodge Spectral Surrogates for Topology-Constrained Optimization
